description:
You are acting as a capacity-planning engineer who has been asked to capture one-off resource-usage snapshots from the current running system and to write a concise report.  
Follow the requirements below exactly.  When the job is complete, every file and directory described here must exist with the exact names, locations, and contents (including character case, line order, number of lines and spacing).

1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/projects/capacity_audit  
   if it does not already exist.

2. Inside that directory produce two plain-text snapshot files that must be named exactly:  
   • cpu_snapshot.txt  
   • mem_snapshot.txt  

   2.1 cpu_snapshot.txt  
       a. The very first line must be the literal header (no extra spaces):  
          PID USER %CPU COMMAND  
       b. Append exactly the next 5 running processes with the highest CPU utilisation, sorted in descending order **by %CPU** at the moment the command is run.  
          – Use the columns in the header and separate them with a single space.  
          – Each of the 5 data lines must therefore have four whitespace-separated columns in the same order as the header.  
          – The file must therefore contain exactly 6 lines.

   2.2 mem_snapshot.txt  
       a. The first line must be the literal header (no extra spaces):  
          PID USER %MEM COMMAND  
       b. Append exactly the next 5 running processes with the highest memory utilisation, sorted in descending order **by %MEM** at the moment the command is run.  
          – Same column and spacing rules as for cpu_snapshot.txt.  
          – The file must therefore contain exactly 6 lines.

3. Derive the current total memory utilisation percentage of the whole system in the same moment you create the snapshots.  
   – Round this single value to **one decimal place** (e.g. 37.4).  

4. Create the file  
   /home/user/projects/capacity_audit/summary_report.log  
   The report MUST contain exactly the five lines below, in the exact order and with no extra blank lines.  
   Replace the capitalised placeholders with the live data you gathered:

   TIMESTAMP=YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  
   TOTAL_MEM_PERCENT=NN.N  
   TOP_CPU_PID=PID1  
   TOP_MEM_PID=PID2  
   SNAPSHOT_FILES=cpu_snapshot.txt,mem_snapshot.txt  

   Requirements for the field values:  
   • TIMESTAMP must be the ISO-8601 UTC time (to the second) recorded at the moment the snapshots were taken, e.g. 2025-04-12T09:15:31Z  
   • TOTAL_MEM_PERCENT must be the value you calculated in step 3 with one decimal place.  
   • TOP_CPU_PID is the PID (first column) from the first data line of cpu_snapshot.txt.  
   • TOP_MEM_PID is the PID (first column) from the first data line of mem_snapshot.txt.  
   • SNAPSHOT_FILES must appear exactly as shown.

5. When finished, you should have exactly these three new files inside /home/user/projects/capacity_audit:  
   cpu_snapshot.txt  
   mem_snapshot.txt  
   summary_report.log  

No additional files should be created.  All file names, line counts, headers, ordering and spacing must match the specifications so the automated grader can validate them precisely.  Do not delete or modify any other files in the system.
truth:
The grader will perform the following checks:

Directory:
- /home/user/projects/capacity_audit must exist and be a directory.

cpu_snapshot.txt:
- Path: /home/user/projects/capacity_audit/cpu_snapshot.txt
- Must exist, be regular file, have 6 newline-terminated lines.
- Line 1 exactly: "PID USER %CPU COMMAND"
- Lines 2-6: each must match regex "^[0-9]+\\s+\\S+\\s+[0-9]+\\.?[0-9]*\\s+.+$"
- Column count on data lines must be 4 when split on one or more spaces.

mem_snapshot.txt:
- Path: /home/user/projects/capacity_audit/mem_snapshot.txt
- Must exist, be regular file, have 6 newline-terminated lines.
- Line 1 exactly: "PID USER %MEM COMMAND"
- Lines 2-6: regex "^[0-9]+\\s+\\S+\\s+[0-9]+\\.?[0-9]*\\s+.+$"
- Column count on data lines must be 4.

summary_report.log:
- Path: /home/user/projects/capacity_audit/summary_report.log
- Must exist, be regular file, have exactly 5 newline-terminated lines.
- Line 1 regex: "^TIMESTAMP=[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$"
- Line 2 regex: "^TOTAL_MEM_PERCENT=[0-9]+\\.[0-9]$"
- Line 3 regex: "^TOP_CPU_PID=[0-9]+$"
- Line 4 regex: "^TOP_MEM_PID=[0-9]+$"
- Line 5 exactly: "SNAPSHOT_FILES=cpu_snapshot.txt,mem_snapshot.txt"

Cross-file consistency:
- The PID on summary_report.log line 3 must equal the PID (first column) on line 2 of cpu_snapshot.txt.
- The PID on summary_report.log line 4 must equal the PID (first column) on line 2 of mem_snapshot.txt.

No other files should exist in /home/user/projects/capacity_audit.
